questions.question: What is the full name of the author of the poem 'Team Work'?
questions.explanation: The poem was written by Edgar Albert Guest (1881-1959).

questions.question: Which of these is the contraction for 'Will not'?
questions.explanation: 'Won't' is the irregular contraction used for the words 'will not'.

questions.question: In a football match, a player loses the ball instead of passing to a teammate. Which line did they forget?
options: Option A: They may sound your praise
Option B: It’s helping your fellowman to score
Option C: You’re a part of a little unit
Option D: The work of life is done
questions.explanation: Teamwork involves selflessness and helping teammates achieve the goal.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'Deed'?
options: Option B: An act or achievement
questions.explanation: In the context of the poem, a 'deed' refers to an action or a specific achievement.

questions.question: Which word rhymes with 'Fame'?
questions.explanation: 'Fame' and 'Game' share the same ending sound.

questions.question: Edgar A. Guest is famously known as the __________.
options: Option B: Poet of the People
questions.explanation: Edgar A. Guest earned the title 'Poet of the People' for his poems about everyday life.

questions.question: What is the most appropriate vocabulary word for a 'plan'?
questions.explanation: 'Scheme' means a plan or a systematic arrangement of work.

questions.question: Expand the contraction: 'He’d'.
options: Option B: He had / He would
questions.explanation: 'He’d' is the shortened form for both 'He had' and 'He would'.

questions.question: Identify the rhyming word for 'Skill'.
questions.explanation: 'Skill' and 'Thrill' end with the same sound.

questions.question: A student lets others speak in a group presentation to look united. What quality is shown?
questions.explanation: Team spirit involves setting aside individual desires for the benefit of the group.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'Mate'?
questions.explanation: In the poem, 'mate' refers to a fellow player or a companion.

questions.question: Which of the following is the contraction for 'I have'?
questions.explanation: The letters 'ha' are omitted and replaced by an apostrophe to form 'I've'.

questions.question: Edgar A. Guest was appointed as the Poet Laureate of which state?
questions.explanation: He is the only poet awarded the title of Poet Laureate of Michigan.

questions.question: What does the poetic abbreviation 'o’re' stand for?
questions.explanation: 'O're' is a poetic contraction used in place of 'over'.

questions.question: What is the rhyme scheme of 'score / seem / o're / team'?
options: Option A: a a b b
Option B: a b a b
Option C: a b b a
Option D: a b c d
questions.explanation: 'Score' rhymes with 'o're' (a), and 'seem' rhymes with 'team' (b), creating a b a b pattern.

questions.question: Which of these is a team game from the lesson's list?
questions.explanation: Basketball requires a group to work together, unlike standard individual sports.

questions.question: According to the poem, what should you do if your teammate is about to score?
options: Option B: Help your fellowman to score
questions.explanation: The poem emphasizes helping teammates score.

questions.question: At what age did Edgar A. Guest begin his career?
questions.explanation: He started his career in 1895 at the age of fourteen.

questions.question: What is the contraction for 'Does not'?
questions.explanation: 'Doesn't' is the correct contraction for 'Does not'.

questions.question: What does 'Spirit' mean in the poem?
questions.explanation: In 'team spirit', it refers to the collective will and determination.

questions.question: Which word rhymes with 'Great'?
questions.explanation: 'Great' and 'Mate' have the same vowel sound.

questions.question: Who does 'They' refer to in 'They may sound your praise'?
questions.explanation: 'They' refers to the public/audience noticing individual performance.

questions.question: A runner drops the baton to finish in style. Did they win the 'game' of teamwork?
options: Option B: No, because they didn't work for the bigger scheme
Option C: Yes, individual fame better
questions.explanation: Victory requires collective effort for the bigger scheme, not individual style.

questions.question: Which publication first featured Edgar A. Guest's work?
questions.explanation: His work first appeared in the 'Detroit Free Press'.

questions.question: Basic qualities needed for a player according to the summary?
questions.explanation: Courage and skill are the fundamental attributes mentioned.

questions.question: Choose the correct contraction for 'There is'.
questions.explanation: 'There's' is the shortened form of 'There is'.

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'Fame'?
questions.explanation: Fame refers to being known or celebrated for achievements.

questions.question: Which of these words rhymes with 'Done'?
questions.explanation: 'Done' and 'Won' rhyme despite different spellings.

questions.question: If you are 'singled out for fame,' it means you are:
questions.explanation: To be 'singled out' means to be recognized apart from the group.

questions.question: A captain attributes a win to the whole team. This concept is:
questions.explanation: Acknowledging the team over self is the essence of team spirit.

questions.question: What is the rhyme for 'Star'?
questions.explanation: 'Star' and 'Are' rhyme perfectly.

questions.question: How many newspapers syndicated Guest's column?
questions.explanation: His column appeared in over 300 newspapers.

questions.question: What is the contraction for 'You will'?
questions.explanation: The contraction for 'You will' is 'You'll'.

questions.question: What is necessary to win the game according to the poem?
options: Option B: Working with your running mate
questions.explanation: Victory is impossible without working with your partner/mate.

questions.question: Which word rhymes with 'Score'?
questions.explanation: 'Score' rhymes with 'o're' (over).

questions.question: Edgar A. Guest's literary style is:
questions.explanation: His poems are positive and uplifting.

questions.question: What does 'It' represent in the context of playing together?
questions.explanation: 'It' represents the concept and spirit of teamwork.

questions.question: Which word rhymes with 'Play'?
questions.explanation: 'Play' and 'Say' are rhyming words.

questions.question: Expand 'Aren't'.
questions.explanation: 'Aren't' is the contraction for 'Are not'.

questions.question: What is the central theme of 'Team Work'?
options: Option A: Winning at any cost
Option B: Importance of unity and selflessness
questions.explanation: The poem emphasizes unity and selflessness over individual glory.
